seoTitle: Gutha vs Gwambygine — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Gutha and Gwambygine. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gutha and Gwambygine.
heading: Gutha vs Gwambygine — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gutha, Western Australia and Gwambygine, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gwambygine has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Gutha (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gutha or Gwambygine safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gutha has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Gwambygine scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gutha scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Gwambygine scores 40/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gutha or Gwambygine?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gwambygine has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
